---
title: "Maidencane (Panicum hemitomon)"
slug: "panicum-hemitomon"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/624291483/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Maidencane"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/624291483/medium.jpg" alt="Maidencane" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/garccyy">
garccyy</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342790026">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=166081)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in freshwater wetlands such as marshes and swamps.

## Natural history notes

A tall, rhizomatous grass characterized by its broad leaves and upright stems that can form dense stands. In Florida, it is commonly found in freshwater wetlands such as marshes, swamps, and along pond margins. It grows during the warmer months and spreads extensively through underground rhizomes, providing important cover and habitat for wildlife.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/166081-Panicum-hemitomon)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/panicum-hemitomon/)
